stories.title: Trump rips NATO allies, dashing European hopes for a kumbaya summit
stories.summary: ANKARA, Turkey — President Donald Trump lashed out at NATO on Tuesday, spoiling the feel-good mood at an alliance summit and reminding allies that he’s not letting go of his grievances. But while Trump rehashed several of his complaints with NATO — including their unwillingness to join the U.S. and Israel in their fight against Iran — he did offer praise for host nation Turkey, again dangling the possibility Ankara would be able to buy sophisticated jet fighters from Washingt

stories.title: ‘Can you help us?’: US oil execs turn to Trump to topple Europe’s climate rules
stories.summary: The U.S. oil and gas industry has succeeded in exporting massive amounts of natural gas to Europe. Now, with the help of White House officials, it looks like it might also succeed in exporting the Trump administration’s deregulatory agenda. Now the sector is attempting to strong-arm the European Commission, the EU’s executive arm, into delaying the rollout of what the bloc intended to be a major rule to curb a potent climate pollution. It has prevailed in winning the backing of over ha

stories.title: US appeals court strikes down key part of Florida law restricting campus race and gender discussions
stories.summary: Ron DeSantis’s Stop Woke Act suffers another legal setback, with the state accused of ‘puppeteering’ Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email A federal appeals panel struck down a significant chunk of Ron DeSantis ’s so-called Stop Woke Act on Tuesday, delivering another rebuff to the Republican Florida governor’s efforts to stifle free speech in higher education. In a scathing order, judges of the 11th circuit court of appeal said by a 2-1 majority that the higher education component o

stories.title: Prosecutors present video of Charlie Kirk’s killing on second day of hearing
stories.summary: Utah county attorney’s office says evidence against suspect Tyler James Robinson is sufficient for case to move to trial Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Utah prosecutors on Tuesday showed video from the day of Charlie Kirk’s killing that they said depicted suspect Tyler James Robinson entering the Utah Valley University campus and climbing on to a roof where they say he fired a fatal shot at the conservative commentator. The Utah county attorney’s office presented the evidence

stories.title: Austrian campaign aims to save writer Stefan Zweig’s Salzburg villa after Porsche tunnel row
stories.summary: Supporters hope to stop 17th-century Villa Europa passing into private hands after Wolfgang Porsche unexpectedly put it on market Austrian cultural figures have launched a campaign to buy a villa once home to the writer Stefan Zweig after its owner, the automotive magnate Wolfgang Porsche, unexpectedly put it on the market following a row over his plans to build a private tunnel for his car collection. Zweig, the Austrian Jewish writer whose novels inspired the Wes Anderson film The Grand Budape

stories.title: Hungarian public media outlets halt broadcasting in post-Orbán shake-up
stories.summary: Péter Magyar hails ‘end of propaganda broadcasts’ as Kossuth radio and M1 TV channels suspend transmission Hungarian public media outlets close to Viktor Orbán have suspended broadcasting, the country’s prime minister said as he hailed efforts to dismantle the longtime nationalist leader’s control over information. Péter Magyar, who ousted Orbán in a landslide election victory in April, wrote on Facebook: “A historic day. Today marks the end of propaganda broadcasts on public media platforms. Th

stories.title: Hungary's public news broadcasts halted in bid to scrap Orban-era propaganda
stories.summary: The country's main state TV channel displayed a message saying it was "sorry" for lying on Tuesday.

stories.title: Sokan számítanak béremelésre az év második felében
stories.summary: Megugrott az infláció feletti béremelésre számítók aránya az év eleje óta a fiatal munkavállalók körében, és feltűnően sokan vannak azok, akik még az idén fizetésemelésre számítanak.

stories.title: Drag queenek is feltűntek Borbély Alexandra filmjének Karlovy Vary-i premierjén, Nagy Ervin elkísérte feleségét
stories.summary: A kulturális államtitkár elkísérte Borbély Alexandrát az elfogadásról szóló cseh versenyfilm premierjére, amiben a színésznő is szerepel. De volt még egy magyar vonatkozás Karlovy Varyban idén.

stories.title: Despite defamation complaint, EP votes not to waive Klára Dobrev's immunity
stories.summary: Fidesz's former minister brought criminal charges against the Hungarian politician last year, arguing that her statements could undermine the public perception of his ministry.

stories.title: Kapitány István felmentette az MVM vezérigazgatóját
stories.summary: Czepek Gábort, az igazgatóság elnökét is menesztette. A döntést közösségi oldalán jelentette be a gazdasági és energetikai miniszter.

stories.title: Beszédet mond Áder János a Sándor-palota elé szervezett tüntetésen
stories.summary: Azt egyelőre még nem tudni, Orbán Viktor felszólal-e a demonstráción.

stories.title: 12 éve nem végeztek karbantartást a szolnoki kórházban lerobbant MR-gép hűtésén
stories.summary: Korábban itt, a Hetényi Géza kórházban ragadt be a liftbe Magyar Péter.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Ma véget ért a propaganda sugárzása a közmédia felületein
stories.summary: A hírszolgáltatás átmenetileg szünetel, olvasható az M1-en, a Kossuth rádió is leállt.

stories.title: A magyar példa miatt nehezedhet a legjobban álló EU-tagjelölt csatlakozása
stories.summary: Az új tagokkal szemben komolyabb demokratikus és jogállamisági garanciákat építenének be az Orbán-effektus megelőzésére.

stories.title: Hungarian public media goes off air
stories.summary: The screen went dark at four o'clock on Tuesday, with only a message of apology for having lied for many years currently being displayed.

stories.title: Ennyire egyszerű – Így sötétült el az állami propagandacsatorna
stories.summary: Egyetlen apró villanás kellett csak hozzá.

stories.title: Kapitány István bejelentette, ki lesz az MVM új vezérigazgatója
stories.summary: Az új vezérigazgató több mint 25 éves energetikai és jelentős felső vezetői tapasztalattal rendelkezik.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor reagált a közmédia leállítására, és elmondta, mit kell most nézni
stories.summary: A volt miniszterelnök nem örül, hogy megszűnik az egyik propagandacsatornájuk.

stories.title: Egyelőre el kell búcsúznunk az Orbán pénteki rádióinterjúit szállító műsortól is
stories.summary: A közmédia ideiglenes műsorrendje szerint egyáltalán nem sugározzák a Jó reggelt, Magyarország!, a Déli Krónika és az Esti Krónika című műsorokat sem.

stories.title: Az egészségügy erején felül teljesített, de ahogy a gazdaságfejlesztést támogattuk, az egy kudarc
stories.summary: Sokan azt hiszik, hogy a több pénz megoldja az állami területek problémáit, pedig legalább olyan fontos, hogy az elköltött pénz jól hasznosuljon. Nem csökkenteni kell, hanem hatékonyabbá kell tenni a bürokráciát az Egyensúly Intézet szakértői szerint. A Magyarország jövő időben új epizódja a hatékony államról.

stories.title: Ha hatékony államot akarunk, el tudod képzelni, hogy egy külföldi szakember vezessen egy hivatalt?
stories.summary: A közutakra vagy a közbiztonságra fordított energia viszonylag jól hasznosult, de van két terület, ahova nyakló nélkül öntötték a pénzt, eredmény viszont alig lett, sőt még romlott is a helyzet. Fontos mondatok az Egyensúly Intézet vezetőivel készült beszélgetésből.

stories.title: KDNP-s képviselő a közmédiás műsorvezetők kirúgásáról: Ilyen a romániai '89-es puccs után sem volt!
stories.summary: Máthé Zsuzsa szerint a Ceaușescu kivégzésével záródó eseménysorozat nem forradalom, hanem puccs volt.

stories.title: Soproni Tamás szerint romlott a közbiztonság Budapesten, a palackvisszaváltási rendszer felfüggesztését kéri
stories.summary: Terézváros polgármestere szerint a kerületben sok lépést tettek a közbiztonság javítása érdekében, de nagyobb a probléma, mint amit egy kerület egyedül meg tudna oldani.

stories.title: Sírva kért bocsánatot az alkalmi szexpartnerét megfojtó ír férfi a bíróságon
stories.summary: Amerikai áldozatának szülei is eljöttek a budapesti tárgyalásra.

stories.title: Elutasította a Kúria Sára Botond érveit, törvényesnek találták Budapest költségvetését
stories.summary: „A főispán szerette volna bebizonyítani, hogy a város költségvetése törvénytelen, de a Kúria most kimondta, hogy nagyon is törvényes, közgazdaságilag megalapozott. Touché” – írta Karácsony Gergely.

stories.summary: Nem akarnak részt venni „a demokrácia leépítésében”.

stories.summary: A fideszes Lezsák Anna az apja érdemeit ecsetelte, amikor a tiszás Kulcsárnak kicsúszott valami a száján.

stories.title: Kielce pogrom in Poland: 80th anniversary of 1946 massacre
stories.summary: Just over a year after World War II ended, a mob in the Polish city of Kielce killed around 40 Jews, some of them Holocaust survivors. A lie about ritual child murder escalated the violence.

stories.title: Revealed: Farage’s £5m gift reported to UK crime agency over money laundering concerns
stories.summary: Exclusive: Latest Guardian revelation about gift from cryptocurrency tycoon comes as Reform UK leader forces byelection The £5m gift to Nigel Farage by a cryptocurrency billionaire was reported to the National Crime Agency by bankers who were concerned it may have been laundered money, the Guardian can reveal. The disclosure will put further pressure on the Reform UK leader, who is awaiting a decision by the standards commissioner over whether his failure to declare the money breached parliament

stories.title: Trump renews call for US to take over Greenland as he arrives for Nato summit
stories.summary: President also threatens to pull all American troops from Europe and rails against Nato stance on Iran war Europe live – latest updates Donald Trump has revived his bid for the US to acquire Greenland, threatening to pull all American armed forces out of Europe after the continent repeatedly pushed back. Arriving at the Nato summit in Ankara on Tuesday, the US president also suggested his commitment to defending Europe had been tempered by political decisions by leaders on immigration and energy

stories.title: UK’s bet on AI will endure under Burnham, says minister
stories.summary: LONDON — AI Minister Kanishka Narayan said AI and technology will continue to be a priority for the U.K. government under its likely next prime minister, Andy Burnham. Speaking at POLITICO’s Playbook Live event on Tuesday, Narayan said he and Technology Secretary Liz Kendall have both met with Burnham “to talk about the centrality” of AI for the U.K. “Of course in the transition, Andy, I’m sure, will bring particular flavor to how he wants to think about technology and AI,” Narayan said — but th

stories.title: Le Pen to run for president despite embezzlement conviction
stories.summary: PARIS — French far-right leader Marine Le Pen announced on Tuesday that she will run for president next year, despite being sentenced to one year of house arrest with an ankle monitor earlier in the day. Le Pen was was found guilty by the Paris Court of Appeals of embezzling funds from the European Parliament. As recently as last week, the far-right leader had said she would not run for president if wearing an ankle monitor. But Le Pen said she will bring the new verdict to a higher court and cl

stories.title: How one US state’s political drama could see the Democrats fumble Congress
stories.summary: A small northeastern state has become ground zero in the United States’ midterm elections — and could determine which party controls Congress during the final years of President Donald Trump’s term. Maine, with a population of 1.4 million, is home to one of the most crucial U.S. Senate races this cycle, in which longtime Republican Senator Susan Collins is running for her sixth term. Democrats had largely aligned behind candidate Graham Platner to challenge her — until Monday, when POLITICO repo

stories.title: Le Pen comeback revives French far right’s biggest question: Can she win?
stories.summary: PARIS — Marine Le Pen’s muted legal victory Tuesday leaves the far-right leader battered and bruised, but it also burnishes her reputation as France’s ultimate political survivor. In an unexpected verdict, a three-judge panel upheld her conviction for embezzlement but reduced her five-year ban on running for office. The judges also sentenced her to a year of house arrest, which she said on Tuesday evening she would appeal. Le Pen’s announcement Tuesday that she will stand as her party’s candidat

Modi’s visit begins on Wednesday evening and marks his third since becom

stories.title: Nato should let Ukraine join to ‘make all of us stronger’, says Zelenskyy
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president argues defensive capabilities built up in war with Russia mean it shouldn’t be excluded Europe live – latest updates Volodymyr Zelenskyy has argued for Ukraine to be allowed to join Nato at its annual summit – saying it would be wrong to exclude a country that had built up strong defences in its long struggle against the Russian invasion. The Ukrainian president said his country had developed almost all the weapons it needed, and now only required European help in developing

stories.summary: Kyiv suffered heavy Russian strikes ahead of the NATO summit in Ankara. Experts say Russian President Vladimir Putin is trying to send a message.

stories.title: Ghana halts visit by South African president amid row over anti-migrant protests
stories.summary: Many in Ghana feared his presence would lead to mass protests, and officials say it is postponed for now.

stories.title: Israeli officer shown throwing stun grenade into car during West Bank raid
stories.summary: Border police officer under investigation after CCTV footage shows him shouting at car’s occupants and throwing device Israeli police have opened an investigation after CCTV footage showed a border police officer throwing a stun grenade into a car carrying young Palestinians during a raid on the Qalandiya refugee camp in the occupied West Bank. The footage from Sunday, released by the Israeli rights group B’Tselem, shows an officer approaching a car and shouting at its occupants. Continue readin

stories.title: What will define Elon Musk’s legacy? Doge cuts to USAID Ebola programs
stories.summary: Experts say cuts have hindered the response to DRC’s Ebola outbreak and resulted in ‘significant numbers’ of deaths Elon Musk has an Ebola problem. SpaceX stock dropped precipitously after its initial public offering, and Tesla faces a wave of lawsuits. But instead of focusing on his companies, Musk has posted frequently on X about the US Agency for International Development (USAID), which he helped dismantle – or, in his words, feed into the woodchipper – last year. “Elon’s USAID crash-out over

stories.title: EU rejects suspending biometric border controls despite 20 ‘difficult spots’
stories.summary: Officials admit new post-Brexit EES system is ‘not perfect’, as airports and airlines voice fears over delays The EU has rejected calls by airports and airlines to suspend the implementation of new fingerprinting and facial recognition border controls even though it admits there are “20 difficult spots” with queue chaos. With only a week to go before the peak summer holiday season starts, EU officials said the new entry/exit system (EES) was “not perfect” but it would tell travel industry repres

stories.title: Bombs explode near Damascus hotel housing Macron on Syria visit
stories.summary: Blasts did not interrupt French president’s visit but are setback for Syrian leaders’ attempt to project stability Explosions rocked Damascus near the hotel where French president, Emmanuel Macron, was staying on Tuesday, wounding at least 18 people, Syrian authorities said. Macron was in the presidential palace for a meeting with the Syrian president, Ahmed al-Sharaa, when two improvised explosive devices detonated near the Four Seasons hotel where Macron was reported to be staying. stories.title: Europe doesn’t need to worry about Andy Burnham. He has the makings of a great ally | Jessica Berlin
stories.summary: Defence strategists fret about the next PM’s inexperience. But he knows about local resilience. That’s the kind of skill Nato urgently needs I recently asked a senior Labour figure what Andy Burnham’s defence agenda might look like. “He doesn’t really have one,” came the reply – offered not so much as a criticism of the UK’s prime minister in waiting, as a statement of fact. Given that Keir Starmer’s resignation came after a mutiny within his own government over inadequate defence spending, some

stories.title: Catnip lotion as effective as Deet at repelling mosquitoes, study finds
stories.summary: Researchers testing a cheap, homegrown oil in Uganda found what cats knew all along – it worked as well as the artificial chemical used globally A homegrown catnip lotion has proven “just as effective as Deet” as a mosquito repellant in trials carried out in Uganda. Catnip, or Nepeta cataria , is a common herb from the mint family. The chemical in the plant that causes feline euphoria – nepetalactone – also has insect-repelling properties but this has not previously been commercialised. Continue

stories.title: Marine Le Pen’s presidential bid hangs in balance as court orders electronic tag
stories.summary: Ban on running for elected office reduced but ankle tag would make campaigning logistically difficult The French far-right leader Marine Le Pen’s 2027 presidential bid is hanging in the balance as she was sentenced to wear an electronic ankle tag after being found guilty of embezzling European parliament funds. stories.title: Bernie Sanders calls on Graham Platner to withdraw from Senate race amid sexual assault claims
stories.summary: Sanders, one of Platner’s earliest and most influential backers, is latest to call on him to withdraw from Maine race The progressive senator Bernie Sanders has called on Graham Platner to withdraw from the US Senate race in Maine, citing “very serious allegations” of sexual assault. While Platner has denied the claim, first reported by Politico, a growing list of Democrats have urged him to stand aside as the party’s candidate. He has said he is “taking the time to reflect on the best path forw

stories.title: Prince Harry and other claimants could face £50m legal bill after losing phone-hacking case
stories.summary: High court dismisses claims by group including Duke of Sussex that Mail publisher used unlawful methods to source stories about them Prince Harry and six other prominent figures are facing a legal bill of up to £50m after losing their case against the publisher of the Daily Mail over claims it used unlawful methods to source stories. In an emphatic ruling that is likely to signal an end to new litigation relating to the phone-hacking scandal era, the high court dismissed all the group’s claims,

stories.title: What does the court ruling mean for Marine Le Pen’s presidential chances?
stories.summary: A look at the the case, the appeal court decision, and how it could affect the race to succeed Emmanuel Macron Marine Le Pen has said she will be a candidate in France’s presidential race next year, after a court ruling on her appeal against a conviction for embezzlement of public funds allowed her to run. However, the path ahead is far from straightforward. Continue reading...

stories.title: Europe faces up to prospect US may be unable to arm Nato allies
stories.summary: Wars in Iran and Ukraine have expended stockpiles of sought-after missiles, leaving gap in military resources There are growing concerns in Europe that the US defence industrial base is no longer providing the weapons pledged to Nato allies with US stockpiles depleted owing to the conflicts in Ukraine and Iran, leaving allies to consider new avenues to arm and defend themselves. As Nato leaders including the US president, Donald Trump, convene in Ankara , Turkey, the US plans to address European

stories.title: Australian PM says Chinese missile test could have caused ‘considerable damage’ if weaponised
stories.summary: Solomon Islands prime minister says he doesn’t want to see more countries testing ICBMs in Pacific, adding ‘be our friend but don’t threaten us’ Australian prime minister Anthony Albanese has said China’s weapons test in the Pacific risks fuelling dangerous nuclear proliferation, with the intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) fired on Monday capable of causing “considerable damage” if weaponised. International condemnation has grown overnight after China’s state news agency Xinhua reported o

stories.title: Germany: Record debt and a seismic policy shift
stories.summary: The German government's draft budget for 2027 includes more money for the military and less for everything else, while debt continues to grow rapidly.

stories.title: Philippines' democracy tested by Duterte impeachment trial
stories.summary: Vice President Sara Duterte faces impeachment in a trial that could shape the 2028 presidential race and test public trust in the country's democratic institutions.

stories.title: Ghana: Uncertainty hangs over new government anti-corruption plan
stories.summary: President John Dramani Mahama wants to build trust by strengthening public accountability. But governance experts say the success of the anti-corruption plan will depend on the consistent enforcement of existing laws.

stories.title: UK: Nigel Farage resigns as MP, to run again in by-election
stories.summary: The best way to deal with growing scrutiny over undeclared financial support is to step down ... and stand again. That's the conclusion Reform UK leader Nigel Farage has come to by forcing a by-election.

stories.title: How drones transformed Iran's Axis of Resistance militias in Lebanon, Gaza, Yemen and Iraq
stories.summary: Drone production, technology transfer and training have enabled Iran's regional allies to operate with far greater independence. Has this network become too resilient to be dismantled through military strikes?

stories.summary: Hundreds of Argentina supporters gathered in Atlanta for a traditional banderazo rally ahead of their World Cup last-16 match against Egypt.

stories.summary: Tibetan Youth Congress activists are detained by police during a 'Free Tibet' protest outside the Chinese Embassy in New Delhi on 7 July 2026.

stories.summary: As reports of the blasts came in, state television said the Syrian president had welcomed Macron at the presidential palace.

stories.summary: The US president's inflammatory words have punctuated the image of unity at this crucial gathering, Security Correspondent Frank Gardner writes.

stories.title: Marine Le Pen to run for French presidency and appeal conviction in top court
stories.summary: The hard-right National Rally leader has ended months of speculation, after a court ruled she could run but would have to wear a tag for a year.

stories.summary: If the Paris Court of Appeal upholds Marine Le Pen's five-year ban from public office, the National Rally figurehead's long-held presidential ambitions would effectively be over.

stories.summary: Prince Harry and several other high-profile claimants have lost a lawsuit against the publisher of the Daily Mail tabloid over allegations of unlawful information gathering. The trial, which began in London’s High Court in January, included accusations against the Associated Newspapers Limited (ANL) company of obtaining information by deception, including phone hacking and corrupt payments. A U.K. High Court judge dismissed all 97 claims, stating the group had “failed to prove their

stories.title: Perth man fighting for life after robot vacuum cleaner explosion
stories.summary: Lachie Perrem suffered life-threatening injuries after the explosion on Thursday destroyed his share house in Brabham Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A Perth man has spent five days fighting for his life after a robot vacuum cleaner explosion set fire to his home. Lachie Perrem, aged in his 20s, was taken to hospital on Thursday with life-threatening burns from a blaze that left his fiancee and housemates without their home. Continue reading...

stories.title: Record wildfires in Europe show failure to adapt carries a mounting cost
stories.summary: Scientists call for better land management alongside reduction in greenhouse gases causing the crisis When storm after storm battered the Mediterranean at the start of the year, drowning fields and sending water spurting from plug sockets , few people were fretting about fires. But just four months later, the murky brown floods that swamped towns and fouled homes across western Europe have given way to angry red blazes and choking black smoke . stories.title: Recession off the cards but Australia faces dreary outlook, economists say
stories.summary: De-escalation of war in the Middle East has lowered oil prices and eased fears of economic downturn but households are still feeling the pinch Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Fears of a recession have fallen away, with economists saying that Australia is set to safely navigate the biggest global oil supply shock in history even as they warn of sub-standard growth in the year ahead. The de-escalation of the Middle East conflict has been accompanied by a major retreat

stories.title: Italian village to impose fines of up to €200 on tourists with bare chests or in swimwear
stories.summary: Varenna’s authorities say wandering around the village shirtless or in swimwear is now banned A fishing village by Lake Como has imposed fines of up to €200 (£170) for people who wander around with bare chests or in swimwear, in the latest attempt by an Italian holiday destination to crack down on uncouth tourists. Varenna has been feeling the strain from an increasing number of visitors and so authorities were moved to introduce new rules aimed at preserving the village’s appearance and guarant

stories.title: Hundreds of jobs at risk as John Lewis announces closure of in-store services
stories.summary: Desks offering gift wrapping and bureau de change stations will be closed at dozens of stores nationwide John Lewis has put 200 jobs at risk as it plans to shut down desks operating gift wrapping and foreign exchange services. The 36-strong department store chain said it had begun a consultation on redundancies as it plans to close the desks that operate bureau de change services in 30 stores, and specialist gift wrapping in 25 stores. Continue reading...

stories.title: Qatar: Iran is legally responsible for hits on three tankers in strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Vessels, including a Qatari LNG carrier, struck close to Oman as Tehran blames US Qatar has warned Iran it will bear full legal responsibility after three tankers, including a Qatari LNG vessel, were struck within hours in the strait of Hormuz. All three were hit close to Oman, which had suggested a new shipping corridor close to its coastline – a proposal Iran opposes as it wants to charge ships to use the waterway. Continue reading...

stories.title: EasyJet’s board has surrendered too easily to US bidder | Nils Pratley
stories.summary: The company’s target to hit £1bn profitability is intact. Why isn’t the board putting up a proper fight? Some foreign takeover swoops on UK listed companies are easier to swallow than others. Sometimes it is hard to mount an argument that shareholders should stick to the virtuous path of independence and say no to an offer of hard cash at a fat premium. stories.title: Prince Harry gets burned in his mission to ‘slay dragons’ of British media

stories.title: Utah revokes license of boarding school where Paris Hilton says she was abused as teen
stories.summary: Hilton in a statement said Provo Canyon school ‘failed the children in its care’ and faced abuse allegations for decades The state of Utah has revoked the license of a boarding school where socialite Paris Hilton said she was abused as a teenager, saying the school had “failed to provide applicable health and safety services for clients”. The state’s action, which took effect on Monday, cites multiple noncompliance issues against the Provo Canyon school’s campus in Springville. The school has 15

stories.title: Labour, Tories and Lib Dems refuse to stand in byelection with Farage’s resignation labelled ‘desperate stunt’ – as it happened
stories.summary: Reform UK leader to quit as MP but stand again, saying the people of Clacton ‘should be the judges of my actions’ Q: Do you think the parliamentary commissioner for standards should investigate Nigel Farage’s gifts from George Cottrell? Badenoch said that was a matter for the commissioner. [Farage is] hinting at press regulation. For all of the criticism and the attacks, and I would even say abuse that I’ve got from the press, I’ve never once recommended curbing our free press. I think this is o

stories.title: Le Pen says she will appeal against conviction and run for French presidency – Europe live
stories.summary: Far-right leader gives TV interview saying that she will appeal against part of today’s ruling which cast doubt on a presidential run The opening speeches are now under way in Ankara, and you can watch them below. This is the Day 1 industry event, not the leaders’ summit, mind you. Continue reading...

stories.title: Sri Lanka prison riot kills 26, with more than 100 others wounded
stories.summary: Victims with cuts and gunshot injuries rushed to hospital after fighting between prisoners from two drug gangs Clashes at a Sri Lankan jail have killed 26 people, including seven guards, and wounded more than 100 in the country’s deadliest prison riot in years, officials said. Victims with cuts and gunshot injuries were rushed to Negombo hospital, north of the capital Colombo after overnight fighting between prisoners from two drug gangs, police said on Monday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Five charged in Liberia after more than 200kg of cocaine seized in drug bust
stories.summary: Shipment discovered at airport in Monrovia and valued at £14.2m had been falsely declared as seasoning cubes Authorities in Liberia have charged five suspects over one of the largest drug seizures in the country’s history, after police found more than 200kg of cocaine falsely declared as Maggi seasoning cubes. The shipment, with an estimated value of $19m (£14.2m), was discovered at the international airport in Monrovia on 8 June, but the suspects were not named until a press briefing at the wee

stories.title: How Volkswagen's huge workforce became a liability
stories.summary: The struggling German carmaker plans to cut up to 100,000 jobs from its 630,000-strong workforce. How did VW's headcount get so bloated, and will the cuts be enough to survive the Chinese EV onslaught?

stories.title: Japan, China coast guards square off near disputed islands
stories.summary: Japanese and Chinese coast guard vessels confronted each other near the disputed Senkaku island chain, with both sides claiming they expelled the other. The incident comes amid rising tensions between Tokyo and Beijing.

stories.title: Syria: 18 injured in Damascus blasts during Macron visit
stories.summary: Explosions occurred near the Four Seasons hotel in the Syrian capital as French President Emmanuel Macron embarked on a landmark visit.

stories.title: India news: Several trapped after deadly Kerala landslide
stories.summary: A landslide in Kerala's Wayanad district killed at least two and injured seven. Officials said more than 10 people could be trapped under the debris. DW has the latest.

stories.title: NATO summit: Allies in Ankara for talks, defense deals
stories.summary: The leaders of NATO's 32 member states are holding two days of talks in the Turkish capital. NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said the summit aims to ensure the military alliance "continues to deliver." DW has more.

stories.title: Germany news: Breweries tap thirst for alcohol-free beer
stories.summary: Germany is producing more non-alcoholic beer as consumer tastes change. Meanwhile, a top court is reviewing a Bavarian law that allows police intervention before a concrete threat has emerged. DW has more.

stories.title: China's missile test builds on Pacific nuclear deterrence
stories.summary: US allies in the Pacific condemned what Beijing has called a "routine" intercontinental ballistic missile test, which comes as China seeks to demonstrate its growing military capabilities.

stories.title: Ukraine: Suspect in Monaco bomb attack found dead near Kyiv
stories.summary: Ukrainain prosecutors say the body of the woman suspected of the bombing that targeted businessman Vadym Yermolaiev was found near Kyiv. Police said they had detained two individuals suspected of murdering her.

stories.title: Heat-resistant housing: How we need to adapt our homes
stories.summary: In Germany, it is time to rethink and rebuild. Many apartments were not designed for heat waves. Fortunately, there are ways to adapt existing buildings to the changing climate — but air conditioning isn't the best.

stories.title: UK judge dismisses Prince Harry's privacy lawsuit
stories.summary: The ruling also scuttles breach of privacy suits brought by six others, including Elton John and Elizabeth Hurley.

stories.title: Conflict, climate change, cash-strapped: Why poverty persists
stories.summary: With just under five years left to complete the UN's Sustainable Development Goals, a new report shows what's hampering progress. And what the solutions are.

stories.title: IOC paves way for Russia's return to Olympics
stories.summary: Russian athletes will be able to take part in qualifying events for the Los Angeles 2028 Olympic Games. It comes two months the IOC had lifted restrictions for Russia's ally Belarus.

stories.title: France: Le Pen vows to run despite upheld graft conviction
stories.summary: After a court appeal rendered her eligible in theory to run while wearing an ankle tag, the far-right leader says she intends to stand next year. She repeated her plan to appeal again, saying the tag may not be needed.

stories.title: NYC high-rise's possible collapse prompts evacuations
stories.summary: New York City's fire chief said the building was in danger of 'localized' collapse, after buckling columns were found.

stories.title: Why migrants come to Germany for work and then leave again
stories.summary: Germany has long attracted skilled workers to its labor market. The country has been much less successful at retaining them. So what is Germany doing wrong?

stories.title: Erika Kirk comes face-to-face in court with husband's alleged killer
stories.summary: Charlie Kirk's widow was joined by the late US activist's family - all of whom attended court for the first time in the case.

stories.title: Macron tours Damascus' Umayyad Mosque during Syria visit
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron visits Damascus' Umayyad Mosque with Syrian leader Ahmed al-Sharaa and signs the guestbook during his Syria trip.

stories.title: South Korean law targeting 'fake news' takes effect as journalists' groups raise concerns
stories.summary: South Korea saw a surge in online misinformation following the botched 2024 martial law attempt, including unsubstantiated allegations of Chinese interference in the electoral system.

stories.title: Euronews launches Kazakh language broadcasting in Astana
stories.summary: As part of its continued development in Central Asia, Euronews now takes the strategic step to launch its new Kazakh language service and bring its European-perspective journalism and global coverage to Kazakhstan.

stories.title: Goats roam free in this French Alpine village
stories.summary: Les Lindarets in the French Alps is known for goats roaming freely through its streets, attracting visitors who feed and interact with the animals.

stories.title: Third tanker hit in Strait of Hormuz in 24 hours, maritime agency says
stories.summary: The UKMTO maritime agency is investigating the incidents and advised vessels in the area to proceed with caution.

stories.title: Lawyer says detained Gaza doctor was severely beaten in Israeli jail

stories.title: Ukraine brands IOC move to lift ban on Russian athletes 'premature' and 'unjustified'
stories.summary: Russian athletes looking to return to international competition will now be able to compete as long as they "meet relevant anti-doping requirements," the IOC said.

stories.title: Woman suspected of Monaco bomb attack found dead in Ukraine

stories.title: Oil tanker hit by 'unknown projectile' in Strait of Hormuz, British military says
stories.summary: Iranian state television said a liquified natural gas tanker came under attack Tuesday in the Strait of Hormuz off Oman after ignoring warnings, implying Tehran carried out the assault on a tanker it said was carrying natural gas from Qatar.

stories.title: Mark Rutte aux alliés de l’Otan : admettez-le, Trump avait raison
stories.summary: ANKARA — Le secrétaire général de l’Otan, Mark Rutte, a un message à adresser aux alliés à propos de Donald Trump : le président américain avait raison. Il avait raison de faire pression sur eux pour qu’ils augmentent leurs dépenses de défense, il avait raison de les inciter à moderniser leurs armées, et il avait raison de déclencher une guerre contre l’Iran, a considéré le Néerlandais auprès de POLITICO lors d’une interview pour le podcast The Conversation with Dasha Burns , enregistrée à l’occ

stories.title: RSF razes multiple villages in Darfur killing dozens as UN warns of rising attacks on children
stories.summary: Sudan’s paramilitary Rapid Support Forces have razed multiple villages in the western regions of the country bordering Chad, killing many and displacing thousands as a separate UN report outlines that over 300 children were killed in their atrocities this year alone.

stories.title: UK charity funding school at heart of illegal Israeli settlement expansion
stories.summary: Friends of Yeshivat Shavei Hevron sent nearly £200,000 to school in Hebron – where Israel has been accused of imposing apartheid – between 2019 and 2024 A British charity is funding a religious school at the heart of expansion plans for the illegal Israeli settlement in the Palestinian city of Hebron. Friends of Yeshivat Shavei Hevron sent nearly £200,000 to the school between 2019 and 2024, the last year for which accounts are publicly available on the website of the Charity Commission, the cha

stories.title: Dowry murders in India no longer spark public anger or debate, study finds
stories.summary: Thousands of women are killed in dowry disputes each year, despite the practice being banned in 1961 Dowry deaths in India no longer provoke the public anger they once did, despite thousands of women’s lives still being lost every year, according to new research. The killings – women who are murdered or driven to suicide following dowry disputes between families – have also faded from political debate, despite an increase in cases. Continue reading...

stories.title: Severe storms in China bring tornadoes and landslides that have killed 15 people
stories.summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping calls for ‘all out’ rescue effort as death toll rises and 16 people ⁠remain buried after a ⁠landslide in the country’s west The death toll from devastating storms in parts of China rose to 15 on Tuesday, with hundreds more injured and tens of thousands evacuated, state media reported, as the country’s leader, Xi Jinping, urged “all out” efforts to rescue people affected by the weather. Thunderstorms and gale-force winds killed at least 11 people and injured 331 in the c

stories.title: Wildfires in southern Europe wreak havoc – in pictures
stories.summary: Wildfires raged across southern Europe, forcing thousands of people to evacuate their homes and prompting officials to ban spectators from a stage of the storied Tour de France cycling race Continue reading...

stories.title: Canada awards Germany major submarine contract
stories.summary: Canadian PM Carney said the deal of up to 12 German-built submarines will be the "largest in Canadian history." Germany had pushed hard for the order, which comes as Canada seeks stronger ties with Europe.

stories.title: Can China repeat its EV success with robotaxis?
stories.summary: China's self-driving car firms have been given a headstart by the country's EV supply chain as they expand globally.

stories.title: Why a Diljit Dosanjh film vanished from streaming after two days
stories.summary: The film's troubled journey spans years of censorship disputes, court battles and an unexpected release on a streaming platform.

stories.title: Why some African nations are turning down Trump aid money

stories.title: Death toll from China storms and floods rises to 15, state media says

stories.title: India's landmark Ram temple overhauls board after alleged theft of donations
stories.summary: Questions have been raised over alleged theft of cash, valuable jewellery, gold and silver offered by devotees.

stories.title: Hong Kong activist Joshua Wong set for September sentencing in second national security case
stories.summary: Wong rose to prominence in Hong Kong in 2012 as a high school student leading protests against the introduction of national education in the city's schools.

stories.title: 'No lasting peace' in the Middle East without a Palestinian state, Egypt's el-Sisi says

stories.title: Giant Lego World Cup trophy unveiled in New York

stories.title: Watch: Moment rare tornado lashes central Chinese cities

stories.title: At least 18 wounded in Damascus blasts during Macron visit, Syrian officials say
stories.summary: Macron had entered the presidential palace to meet Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa when the explosions happened near the Four Seasons Hotel.

stories.title: San Fermín festival begins with Pamplona's famous bull run

stories.summary: A pártcsaládokat felügyelő szervezetnek vizsgálnia kell, hogy az ESN tiszteletben tartja-e az európai értékeket. A szervezet főtitkára korábban ezt Toroczkaira is hivatkozva kétségesnek látta.

stories.title: Ukrajnában holtan találták a monacói robbantás fő gyanúsítottját

stories.summary: A beruházások terén viszont csak mérsékelt élénkülés várható.

stories.summary: A drónok alapvetően átírták a hadviselést, mondta az ukrán elnök Ankarában. Zelenszkij szerint az Oroszország ellen ötödik éve védekező ország dróntechnológiája és háborús tapasztalata olyan fontos, hogy vétek lenne kihagyni a NATO-ból.

stories.summary: Azonnali hatállyal felmentették a munkavégzés alól. Az MTVA több vezetőjével is ez történt. A székházban rossz a hangulat.

stories.summary: A délutáni híradóban emlékeztek meg az aktualitásokról.

stories.title: Több mint egy évtizedes patthelyzet után szinte egyhangúlag szavazta meg az EP a légiutas-jogi reformot
stories.summary: A BEUC európai fogyasztóvédelmi szervezet nemrég úgy látta, a fogyasztók örülhetnek a leendő szabályoknak, a Ryanair szerint ezek még hülyébbek lesznek.

stories.summary: A brexit atyja, aki a felméréseket vezető Reform UK vezetője, azt reméli, hogy újraválasztják, és ezáltal megerősítik pozíciójában.

stories.title: Jogtalanul bírságolta 10 millióra Kövér Szabó Tímeát a strasbourgi bíróság szerint
stories.summary: A párbeszédes politikus 5 év után pert nyert az egykori házelnökkel szemben az Emberi Jogok Európai Bíróságán.

stories.summary: Kedden 16 órától 19:56-ig sötét képernyő fogadta a nézőket és a látogatókat, rajta bocsánatkérő felirattal.

stories.summary: A szociális szabályok frissítésénél a Fidesz, a Mi Hazánk és a Tisza is tartózkodott az EP-ben. Utóbbi szerint a szöveg több ponton kedvező, de nem ad minden kérdésre megnyugtató választ.

stories.summary: A FIFA fenntartja, hogy kiegyensúlyozott döntés volt az amerikai Balogun eltiltásának felfüggesztése.

stories.summary: Korábban azt írták az utasoknak, számla ellenében járni fog a kompenzáció a transzferköltségek után.

stories.summary: De állítják, ettől még ugyanúgy elítélik az oroszok ukrajnai invázióját, ahogyan eddig, és szolidárisak is az ukránokkal.

stories.summary: Micheál Martin az uniós kormányok testületének elnökségi programját vázolta, Gál Kinga az „előző, patrióta, szuverenista kormány évekig tartó alaptalan vegzálását” is felemlegette.

stories.summary: Torstein Træen benne volt a nap szökésében, miközben Pogačar lazított a negyedik szakaszon, amelyet Mads Pedersen nyert meg.

stories.title: A tanúval indult újra az M1 műsora
stories.summary: Mi mással.

stories.summary: Az argentinok csapatkapitánya mélyen átélte a futball-vb eddigi legnagyobb fordítását. Gólja óriási energiákat szabadított fel benne.

stories.title: A 2029-es EP-választáson már önállóan mérettetné meg magát a KDNP egy vitaanyag szerint

stories.summary: A HírTV beszámolt arról, hogy a Védvonal tüntetést hirdetett az MTVA székháza elé. Alig mentek el, erre a Fidesz közleményt adott ki, hogy ez a Védvonal nem is az a Védvonal.

stories.title: Az utolsó negyeddöntős hely a tét: Svájc–Kolumbia 0-0

stories.title: China tests long-range missile in South Pacific in move Australia condemns as ‘destabilising to region’
stories.summary: Launch comes just hours after Australia and Fiji sign defence agreement as expert says timing not a coincidence China has conducted a long-range missile test in the South Pacific just hours after Australia signed a defence agreement with Fiji, sparking condemnation from Canberra and regional leaders. The Australian foreign minister, Penny Wong, said the missile test was “destabilising” to the region, while her New Zealand counterpart, Winston Peters, described it as “deeply concerning”. Continue

stories.title: Cuban zoo celebrates birth of Bengal tigers amid energy crisis
stories.summary: Arrival of endangered cats, including rare white cub, revitalises team straining under fuel and medicine shortages For the Cuban zookeeper Ángel Cordero, the sight of four Bengal ⁠tiger cubs playing in a cage at the Cuban national zoo is a small miracle on an island stifled by shortages ⁠of fuel, medicine and ⁠days-long power outages. The ​birth of these endangered big cats – including an exceedingly rare white tiger – has revitalised a team of zoo workers, he said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canada to buy 12 hi-tech German submarines after bidding war
stories.summary: TKMS beats South Korean rival to multibillion-dollar contract that will deepen Canada’s Nato ties Canada has selected a German consortium to build a dozen cutting-edge submarines in one of the country’s largest-ever defence contracts that will further deepen its Nato ties before a crucial summit this week. On Monday the prime minister, Mark Carney, announced the winner of a tightly contested battle for the lucrative government contract to replace its fleet of ageing, secondhand subs, most of whi

stories.title: Millions join funeral procession for Iran’s supreme leader Ali Khamenei
stories.summary: Crowds swelled through Tehran as mourners dressed in black carried flags proclaiming: ‘We will rise’ A crowd of “millions” assembled on Monday for the funeral procession of Iran’s assassinated supreme leader, Ali Khamenei . The scale and depth of the march, however engineered, represents an extraordinary turnaround for a country that only seven months ago was gripped by street protests at which thousands of people were killed by government security forces. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Living like this is agony’: Cuba suffers third nationwide blackout in six months
stories.summary: Impoverished island was already struggling to keep the lights on before the US imposed a blockade in January Cuba on Monday suffered its third nationwide power outage since the start of the year, the state electricity company said. The impoverished island was already struggling to keep the lights on before the US president, Donald Trump, imposed an oil blockade in January, which has depleted the already dwindling supply of fuel for Cuba’s power plants. Continue reading...

stories.title: At least 26 killed and more than 100 wounded in Sri Lanka prison riot, officials say

stories.title: Rodríguez defends Venezuela’s emergency earthquake response as number of bodies expected to soar

Many Venezuelans have expressed anger at what they see as the US-backed government’s inadequate response to the 24 June disaster before international teams arrived. Continue reading...

stories.title: Typhoon Maysak kills two and forces thousands to evacuate in China
stories.summary: Tropical storm causes extreme flooding in south of the country with heavy rainfall expected in coming days A tropical storm has killed two people, caused dam breaches and forced tens of thousands to evacuate in southern China. Typhoon Maysak killed two people in Nanning, in China’s southern Guangxi province. Maysak, which lashed Vietnam and China’s southern island province of Hainan over the weekend, will dump the water it sucked up on its way ⁠across the South China Sea as it weakens and heads

stories.title: European space telescope Euclid spots oldest quasars yet
stories.summary: Think of quasars as time machines. Rare but revealing. They are light from the beginnings of the universe that let astronomers investigate how early galaxies formed.

stories.title: Chinese Tianwen-2 space probe reaches asteroid for sampling
stories.summary: After a yearlong flight of a billion kilometers, a Chinese space probe is set to take samples from an asteroid. The mission is part of what President Xi Jinping calls the country's "space dream."

stories.title: Firefighters battle wildfires across southern Europe
stories.summary: Wildfires are raging across several parts of southern Europe. They come on the heels of massive heat waves in much of the region.

stories.title: Austrian court convicts 2 former Syrian officials of abuse
stories.summary: A Vienna court has convicted two former Syrian security officials, including the former intelligence chief in Raqqa, for offenses including torture and sexual assault against opponents of Bashar Assad's regime.

stories.title: Emmanuel Macron visits Syria, 18 months after Assad's fall
stories.summary: The French president landed in Damascus on Monday evening. He's the first EU leader to visit the country since Bashar Assad's sudden downfall and the rise of former militant turned President Ahmed al-Sharaa.

stories.title: From 'a beautiful night' to 'world's greatest hangover', guests react to Taylor and Travis's wedding
stories.summary: Some of the famous guests have been posting on social media about the big day in New York.

stories.title: 'Major damage' as Super Typhoon Bavi makes landfall on US Pacific islands
stories.summary: The eye of the Category 5 super typhoon passed over the tiny island of Rota Monday morning local time, bringing winds of more than 241 km/h, also hitting the nearby islands of Guam, Tinian and Saipan.

stories.title: Activists covered in red paint protest bullfighting in Pamplona
stories.summary: Animal rights activists covered in red paint protest bullfighting in Pamplona before the San Fermin Festival, calling for an end to the tradition.

stories.title: Ali Khamenei's coffin carried through the streets of Tehran
stories.summary: Thousands of mourners gathered in Tehran as Iran began funeral ceremonies for late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

stories.title: Girl trapped in Venezuela quake 'survived on ketchup and cheese'
stories.summary: BBC international correspondent Yogita Limayee meets 12-year-old Fabiana, who was trapped in Venezuela's devastating earthquakes.

stories.title: Mexico fans left heartbroken as World Cup dream comes to an end
stories.summary: Mexico fans in Mexico City react with joy and heartbreak as England win 3-2 in a World Cup last-16 match, ending Mexico’s tournament run.

stories.title: Watch: Why Australia's PM apologised for 'inappropriate' Kylie comments
stories.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has apologised for remarks he made about popstar Kylie Minogue in a podcast interview last week.

stories.title: Joy for England fans after dramatic World Cup victory
stories.summary: England fans celebrated their team's dramatic 3-2 victory over Mexico as the Three Lions booked a place in the World Cup quarter-finals.

stories.title: Hamas announces dissolution of Gaza governing body, paves way for technocratic committee
stories.summary: The move marks a significant political shift by Hamas, which has run Gaza since its fighters seized control from rival Palestinian movement Fatah in 2007.

stories.title: Wildfire rages in eastern Spain, forcing hundreds to flee
stories.summary: A wildfire in Castellon, eastern Spain, forces 500 evacuations as firefighters and aircraft battle flames spreading across the Valencia region.

stories.title: Watch: Russian attack leaves gaping hole in Kyiv residential building
stories.summary: The BBC's Sarah Rainsford is at the scene of one of the blasts to hit Ukraine's capital overnight.

stories.title: Wildfire in southern France forces evacuation of 10,000 people
stories.summary: Tour de France organisers ban spectators from stage three as a wildfire hits the Pyrénées-Orientales region.

stories.title: Sri Lanka prison riots leave 26 dead and more than 100 injured
stories.summary: The two days of violence at Negombo Prison are the worst prison riots in the country in years.

stories.title: 'The spectacle Iran wants the world to see': Lyse Doucet in Tehran
stories.summary: Huge crowds have lined the streets of Tehran for the funeral procession of Iran's late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

stories.title: 'It's a very sad day' - Mexican fans react to loss to England
stories.summary: Mexico's World Cup campaign ended with a 3-2 defeat to England in Mexico City.

stories.title: Ex-Syrian intelligence chief found guilty of torture and sexual abuse by Austrian court
stories.summary: Two former Syrian officials went on trial in Vienna, Austria, accused of crimes against opponents of former Syrian leader Bashar al-Assad

stories.title: More than 1,000 arrested as part of global human trafficking crackdown
stories.summary: Interpol says the vast majority of the 2,070 victims or potential victims identified were women being trafficked for sex.

stories.title: Artists create giant sea animals from sand
stories.summary: Artists create large sand sculptures of marine life on Rab island in Croatia during the Festival of Sand Sculptures, turning the beach into a temporary exhibition.

stories.title: Albania PM Rama: Protests a stage for anti-Trump forces
stories.summary: Protesters in Albania say their government is selling the country its nature out to foreign investors and luxury developers. Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama tells DW why he thinks people are so upset.

stories.title: Archaeologists uncover ancient Byzantine city in Egypt’s western desert
stories.summary: Well-preserved fourth-century quarters reveal details of daily life, urban development and economic activities Archaeologists in Egypt have uncovered a well-preserved Byzantine-era city in the western desert. The fourth-century quarters had residential and religious structures, including a basilica-style church in the Dakhla oasis. Archaeologists also found coins, pottery fragments and tools. Continue reading...

stories.title: Founder of prominent underground church released from prison in China
stories.summary: Ezra Jin, founder of Zion Church, lands in the US and reunites with family who are ‘overwhelmed with joy’ The founder of one of China’s most prominent underground churches has been released from prison and reunited with his family in the United States. Ezra Jin, the founder of Zion Church, landed in the US on Friday evening. He was one of dozens of church members who were detained in a sweeping crackdown on Christians in October . Continue reading...

stories.title: German riot police clash with protesters attempting to block far-right AfD conference – video
stories.summary: Riot police have clashed with opponents of the far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) party on the streets of Erfurt in Germany, where thousands met to block roads and prevent AfD delegates from attending the party’s biennial national conference to elect its leadership. Police reported 20,000 protesters were demonstrating in the eastern city, where Alice Weidel and Tino Chrupalla are expected to be re-elected as the party’s co-leaders in the run-up to crucial regional elections in which AfD

stories.title: Jehovah's Witnesses fight German state for Holocaust archive
stories.summary: The Jehovah's Witnesses have secured a partial victory in a dispute over a unique archive documenting the community's persecution by the Nazi regime. It comes after a new memorial to the victims was unveiled in Berlin.

stories.title: Why US train travel lags behind Asia and Europe
stories.summary: As flights get pricier and gas prices stay high, more US travelers are turning to trains — and hitting a system built for freight, not people.

stories.title: Wildfires rage in Portugal, Greece, France and Spain
stories.summary: Six countries in southern Europe have been hit by wildfires. One of the blazes, in southwestern France, could affect Monday's third stage of the Tour de France, authorities warn.

stories.title: Far-right AfD reaffirms ambition to govern Germany
stories.summary: The AfD has strengthened its hardline leadership despite ongoing scrutiny from Germany's domestic intelligence agencies. Co-leader Alice Weidel says the far-right party is ready to govern after strong polling gains.

stories.title: Fireworks light up Washington sky for America's 250th anniversary
stories.summary: A fireworks display illuminated the National Mall in Washington as the United States marked the 250th anniversary of its independence.

stories.title: 'The American Dream is back,' Trump says during Fourth of July speech
stories.summary: "Our warriors did not fight communism on battlefields across the world only to have that menace rear its ugly head right back here in America," Trump told a cheering crowd.

stories.title: Zelenskyy and Putin hold Fourth of July phone calls with Trump
stories.summary: Yuri Ushakov, a Russian presidential aide, said Putin and Trump discussed "the issue of the Ukrainian settlement" in a phone call that lasted an hour and twenty-five minutes.

stories.title: Eulogist at Khamenei funeral in Iran calls for death of Donald Trump
stories.summary: Trump said in January that he had given “very firm instructions” to “wipe them off the face of this Earth” if Iran ever tried to assassinate him.

stories.title: Cargo ship reports being attacked in the Red Sea, maritime agency says
stories.summary: "Vessels are advised to transit with caution and report any suspicious activity to UKMTO," the agency said.

stories.title: JD Vance says 'beautiful' Britain has been 'failed by its leadership'
stories.summary: While he called Britain "amazing," Vance said there appeared to him to be something "very broken about British politics".
